rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF SARAH J. CAHILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Specially Appearing Respondents Patrick Cahill, John Sullivan, And The Northern Trust Company Of Delaware'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Petition Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 4] 2. Strict Compliance with Service Statutes Even if a party has sufficient minimum contacts with the forum state to create a basis for jurisdiction, jurisdiction must be acquired by service of process in strict compliance with the requirements of California's service statutes. (Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup. Ct (1988) 206 Cal. App. 3d 1222, 1229.) The court disagrees that the out-of-state Respondents are entitled to personal service of the Petition pursuant to Probate Code section 850 et seq. (see, Probate Code section 851(a); Code of Civil Procedure sections 413.10, 415.40, 415.95(a), and 417.20.) The Notice of Hearing indicates service by first-class mail. However, there is no signed return receipt or other evidence satisfactory to the court establishing actual delivery to each of the Respondents, proffered to the court on this record (see Code of Civil Procedure 415.40, 417.20.) Subsequent code-compliant service does not salvage deficient initial service or moot a motion to quash for lack of jurisdiction. The Motion to Quash on the basis of defective service is GRANTED. 3. Forum Non Conveniens Respondents also move, alternatively, under CCP section 410.30 to stay or dismiss the action for forum non conveniens. In light of the court's ruling on the Motion to Quash, the court need not address this issue. In the Opposition, Petitioner requests the opportunity to amend the Petition, in order to sufficiently plead jurisdictional facts and cure the Petition's current deficiencies. The court DENIES the request for leave to amend. Specially-appearing Respondents are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. [Part 3 of 4]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF SARAH J. CAHILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Specially Appearing Respondents Patrick Cahill, John Sullivan, And The Northern Trust Company Of Delaware'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Petition Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 4] To meet the burden on a motion to quash for lack of jurisdiction, Petitioner must submit "competent evidence in affidavits and authenticated documentary evidence." (Jewish Def. Org. v. Superior Court (1999) 72 Cal. App. 4th 1045, 1054-55.) Declarations are insufficient if they consist of "vague assertions of ultimate facts" (Id.); verified statements are insufficient if made on mere "information and belief." (Shearer v. Super. Ct. (1977) 70 Cal. App. 3d 424, 430, "information and belief" allegations in verified complaint do not "sustain the burden" on a motion to quash). Here, the Petition is verified by Petitioner under penalty of perjury in accordance with CRC 7.103 ("All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified"). The Opposition is also verified by Petitioner. As the Motion points out, however, several of the material allegations and factual assertions in the verified Petition and Opposition are made only "on information and belief" or are legal conclusions not supported by specific facts or "declarations by competent witnesses." On the record before the court, Petitioner has not met the initial burden to establish by a preponderance of admissible evidence facts justifying the exercise of jurisdiction over each of the Respondents. Petitioner has not shown on this record that each required element allowing a court to exercise specific personal jurisdiction over each nonresident party has been met: (1) "the defendant has purposefully availed himself or herself of forum benefits" [citation]; (2) "the 'controversy is related to or "arises out of "[the] defendant's contacts with the forum'" [citation]; and (3) "'the assertion of personal jurisdiction would comport with" fair play and substantial justice "'" [citation.]. The allegations which seek to meet each of these three elements are not supported by a "preponderance of admissible evidence," and are further contradicted by competent evidence proffered by Respondents. Petitioner also fails to adequately address the 2017 Order entered by this court in related case number PTR-16-300408, transferring the situs of Trust administration out of California, with the signed consent of Petitioner and the residual beneficiaries. On this record, specially-appearing Respondent Patrick Cahill does not have sufficient minimum contacts with California as a forum state to create a basis for jurisdiction. On this record, the specially-appearing Respondent named as the institutional co-trustee as of 2018, Northern Trust of Delaware, a legal entity incorporated and located in Delaware, does not have sufficient minimum contacts with California as a forum state to create a basis for jurisdiction. On this record, specially-appearing Respondent John Sullivan does not have sufficient minimum contacts with California as a forum state to create a basis for jurisdiction, in either his personal capacity as a resident of New Jersey, nor in his capacity as an employee of Northern Trust of Delaware. The Motion to Quash on the basis of lack of personal jurisdiction is GRANTED. [Part 2 of 4]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF SARAH J. CAHILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Respondents Patrick Cahill, John Sullivan, And The Northern Trust Company Of Delaware'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Petition Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Or Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 4] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is specially-appearing Respondents Patrick Cahill, John Sullivan, and the Northern Trust Company of Delaware's Motion to Quash, or in the Alternative, to Stay or Dismiss for Forum Non Conveniens. Petitioner's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The Court, on its own motion, also takes Judicial Notice of the records of this court filed in In re Sarah Cahill Trust, case number PTR-16-300408, and In the Matter of the Sarah and Patrick Cahill Trust, case number CPF-89-911439. (Evid. C. section 452.) Respondents' objection to the procedurally defective Opposition to Motion, on the basis of violation of the page limit under CRC, rule 3.1113(d) is OVERRULED. The court has reviewed the over-length Opposition, in exercise of its discretion. The parties are cautioned, however, procedural defects in filings may properly be the basis to deny a motion. Statutory procedural requirements must be followed by all parties to an action, as a function of due process and the efficient function of the court. The motion is made under CCP sections 418.10 and 410.30, on the grounds that the Court lacks personal jurisdiction over Respondents. 1. Personal Jurisdiction Under CCP section 418.10(a), A defendant, on or before the last day of his or her time to plead or within any further time that the court may for good cause allow, may serve and file a notice of motion for one or more of the following purposes: (1) To quash service of summons on the ground of lack of jurisdiction of the court over him or her. [] The parties do not appear to dispute that the Court does not have general jurisdiction over Respondents, as they are non-residents. Personal jurisdiction over a nonresident defendant depends upon two elements: (1) the defendant must have sufficient minimum contacts with the forum state to create a basis for jurisdiction; (2) if that basis exists, jurisdiction must be acquired by service of process in strict compliance with the requirements of California's service statutes. (Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup. Ct (1988) 206 Cal. App. 3d 1222, 1229.) Per the California Supreme Court in Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 444, a court may exercise specific personal jurisdiction over a nonresident defendant only if a three-prong test is met: (1) "the defendant has purposefully availed himself or herself of forum benefits" [citation]; (2) "the 'controversy is related to or "arises out of "[the] defendant's contacts with the forum'" [citation]; and (3) "'the assertion of personal jurisdiction would comport with "fair play and substantial justice"'" [citation.] The Petitioner has the initial burden to make a sufficient showing on these prongs. "When a defendant moves to quash service for lack of personal jurisdiction, the plaintiff must establish by a preponderance of the evidence the facts justifying the exercise of jurisdiction. The burden then shifts to the defendant to demonstrate exercising jurisdiction would be unreasonable." (Buskirk v. Buskirk (2020) 53 Cal. App. 5th 523, 530, quoting Jayone Foods, Inc. v. Aekyung Industrial Co. Ltd. (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 543, 553.) "Jurisdictional facts must be proved by admissible evidence. This generally requires declarations by competent witnesses. A properly verified complaint may be treated as a declaration for this purpose." (Buskirk v. Buskirk (2020) 53 Cal. App. 5th 523, 535, quoting Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2020) 3:387.) [Part 1 of 4]
